methods.description: Process for preparing and analyzing chironomid subfossil head capsules from sediment samples to study past aquatic environments.

steps.description: A total of 33 samples (approximately 2 g per wet sample) were collected from the Hodon sedimentary core at 35 cm intervals for chironomid subfossil analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from Hodon core at 35 cm intervals

steps.description: Sediment samples were flocculated in a 10% solution of KOH with a water bath at 70 °C for 20 min, and then washed through a 90 μm sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: flocculate and sieve sediment samples using KOH solution

steps.description: Chironomid head capsules were picked from the residual material using fine forceps under a stereo-zoom microscope at ×25 magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: extract chironomid head capsules using microscope

steps.description: All head capsules were mounted on microscope slides using Hydromatrix ®.
steps.searchable_summary: mount chironomid head capsules on slides

steps.description: The chironomid head capsules were identified mainly following the identification methods of Wiederholm and Brooks.
steps.searchable_summary: identify chironomid head capsules using standard methods

methods.method_name: Ordination Analysis and Regime Shift Detection
methods.description: Statistical analysis using principal component analysis (PCA) and STARS analysis to detect shifts in aquatic communities over time.

steps.description: The principal component analysis of the 28 taxa with Hill's N2 > 2 shows that the first two principal component axes explained 39.05% of the total variability and the PCA 1 accounted for 20.8%.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on taxa data to explain variability

steps.description: According to the STARS analysis, the PCA 1 sample scores for chironomids indicates that a significant shift in the aquatic communities of Hodon sedimentary core occurred at ~9 cal kyr BP.
steps.searchable_summary: use STARS analysis to detect regime shift at 9 cal kyr BP
